response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior full-stack developer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby) and a solid testing/craftsmanship culture, but they fall well short of the role's hard requirements. C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — far from the required 8 years — and their career centers on web/product engineering in TypeScript and Node.js rather than cloud compute platforms, storage, or hardware-adjacent infrastructure. Formal technical-leadership experience mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers on large-scale platform strategy is also not evidenced.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (micro-services at millions of HTTP requests/sec using async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus authored test frameworks and taught testing courses
Broad polyglot background and self-driven ownership (founded and operates an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes)
concerns: C++ experience (~7 months professional) is drastically below the required 8 years
No demonstrated experience building or leading a cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure team
Technical leadership is informal (workshops, mentoring, founding a small project) rather than cross-team platform leadership with roadmap and delivery ownership
